Defendant Concannon Construction, Inc.'S Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement.
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Howard Olsen, Esq., Law Offices of Michael Heath, Telephone, 415-931-4207, appearing for Concannon Construction, Inc.;
Jeremy Snider, Esq., Bonetati & Soble, Inc., Telephone 714-466-6600, appearing for Cross-Defendant David Ramirez Construction; (CourtCall)
Jill J. Lifter, Esq., Ryan & Lifter, Telephone, 925-884-2080, appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Newell & Company, Inc., et al; (CourtCall)

Ruling - ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Concannon Construction, Inc.'s Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement.

Cross-Defendant Concannon Construction, Inc.'s motion for an order determining good faith settlement is granted. The Court has carefully considered the declarations and documents filed by all three cross-defendants seeking good faith determinations in this case, particularly Plaintiff's interrogatory responses regarding the amount of their claimed damages and the report of Plaintiffs' expert Richard Avelar regarding the causes of the claimed damages and the costs to repair the identified water intrusion and associated damages. The discovery responses, expert report, and other declarations provide sufficient information to support a rough approximation of the Plaintiffs' total recovery and of the moving party's proportionate liability. The Court finds that the amount of the settlement is within the reasonable range of the settling tortfeasor's proportional share of comparative liability for the plaintiff's injuries, and that the other pertinent factors weigh in favor of granting the motion. (See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Based on information available at the time of the settlement, it is reasonable to conclude that in light of the moving party's limited role in the construction of Plaintiffs' house and the apparent lack of connection between its work and the causes of the water intrusion and associated damages, the moving party had little, if any, potential liability for Plaintiff's claimed damages. (See Cahill v. San Diego Gas & Electric Co. (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 939, 962-966 [settlement amount of $25,000 was within ballpark of good faith settlements under Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6, despite plaintiff's multi-million dollar damages claim, when plaintiff had little if any chance of recovery at trial].) Prevailing party to submit form of order that comples with CRC 3.1312.

Off Calendar Jul-17-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jordan Rodman, Esq. 707-278-9878 of Rodman & Asscoiates for CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT PETER QUIRKE.
Rachel Ostrander, Esq. 831-655-8822 of Lowa Ball & Lynch for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Newell Compnay through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D.The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT PETER QUIRKE'S Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Cross-defendant Peter Quirke's motion for determination of good faith of his settlement with plaintiff Jeffrey Klein is denied without prejudice to being refiled based on a better evidentiary record. Mr. Quirke again fails to present substantial evidence showing a rough approximation of plaintiff's recovery and Mr. Quirke's proportionate liability at the time of the settlement in September of 2016. The addition of the declarations of Mr. Price and Mr. Stokes do not cure the evidentiary gap that existed in the prior good faith motion. At most, they show that Mr. Klein incurred $342,424 in repair costs. There is insufficient information regarding a total approximation of Mr. Klein's damages, including consequential damages such as loss of use damages, that existed at the time of the settlement.

Counsel for Newell & Co. is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
